fed_stance.input: Neutral hold (99.3% prob no change Mar 18-19)

real_yields.input: Falling (FRED DFII10 1.82% as of Mar 10, down from ~2.0% in Jan - confirmed FALLING >5bps)

gold_etf_flows.input: Inflows (9 consecutive months, record $701B AUM, SSGA Feb +$4.5B)

GC.note: Total weighted +8 = STRONG_BULLISH per signal table (>=+5). Score set to 3 (PM code). KEY CHANGE from 0230 session: DFII10 confirmed FALLING (1.82%, down from ~2.0% in Jan), reversing the prior report's stale 'Rising' assumption. Falling real yields (weight 2) are the dominant bullish driver. Gold ETF inflows confirmed strong (9 consecutive months, record AUM). Geopolitical risk-off and oil supply disruption add further support.

SI.note: Total weighted +5 = STRONG_BULLISH per table (>=+5). Score set to 2 (SLIGHT_BULLISH PM code) given silver's hybrid precious/industrial nature and copper being flat. Silver is less pure a beneficiary of falling real yields than gold. Confidence 6/10 due to copper neutrality limiting industrial demand support.

oil_supply_shock.input: Tightening/Disruption (US-Iran war active, IEA recommending reserve release, WTI $90.77)

inventories.input: Build (+3.8M barrels week ending Mar 6, 2026 per EIA)

geopolitical_risk.input: Rising (US-Iran war, Iran threatening $200/bbl, oil +17% from pre-war levels)

CL.note: Total weighted +4 = BULLISH per signal table. Score set to 2 (PM code for BULLISH). Supply disruption (weight 2) and geopolitical risk are dominant bullish drivers. Inventory build (-1) is a partial offset. Note: CL growth_narrative score is +1 (slowing) per methodology lookup table - slowing growth = +1 for CL as it reduces demand concerns less than supply shock amplifies price.

credit_spreads.input: Widening (HY OAS 3.06%, rising from recent lows, Iran war risk)

ES.note: Total weighted +4 = BULLISH per signal table. However, VIX is RISING (+2.85%) and credit spreads are WIDENING — both bearish signals. Falling real yields (weight 2, +4) are the dominant positive factor. Score set to -1 (SLIGHT_BEARISH PM code) because the risk-off environment (VIX rising, credit widening) overrides the yield tailwind in the near term. Equities face a tug-of-war: falling real yields are structurally bullish but rising VIX and geopolitical risk cap upside today.

NQ.note: Total weighted +5 = STRONG_BULLISH per table (>=+5). Score set to 1 (SLIGHT_BULLISH PM code) given MOVE index is sharply rising (+23.54% 1mo), indicating elevated rate volatility that is a headwind for long-duration tech. Falling real yields (weight 2, +4) are the primary bullish driver. SOX is flat (not confirming a strong tech rally). Confidence 6/10 due to MOVE headwind and risk-off environment.

YM.note: Total weighted +5 = STRONG_BULLISH per table (>=+5). Score set to 1 (SLIGHT_BULLISH PM code) given the conflicting signals: credit spreads are widening (bearish) and VIX is rising. YM benefits from steepening yield curve (financials/value) and slowing growth narrative (weight 2). Lower rate sensitivity than NQ makes it relatively more resilient. Confidence 5/10 due to mixed signals.

RTY.note: Total weighted +4 = BULLISH per table. Score set to -1 (SLIGHT_BEARISH PM code) per RTY methodology notes: RTY is most sensitive to credit conditions. Widening credit spreads (weight 2, -2) are the key headwind for small caps. Per RTY cheat card: 'Bearish: Credit stress, regional bank issues'. Credit widening in a geopolitical risk environment warrants bearish lean despite the yield tailwind.

eurozone_growth.input: Stable (Mfg PMI 50.8, Services PMI 51.9 in February; Europe more exposed to oil shock)

M6E.note: Total weighted -1 = NEUTRAL per table. Score set to -1 (SLIGHT_BEARISH PM code) given directional EUR/USD downtrend from 1.20 to 1.1526. Rate differential (weight 2, -2) is the dominant driver. ECB at 2.00% vs Fed at 3.50-3.75% = 150-175bps gap favoring USD. Risk-off provides a partial EUR offset (+1) but insufficient to overcome rate differential headwind.

rba_stance.input: Hawkish (RBA hiked to 3.85% Feb 3, 2026; all 4 major banks expect 2+ more hikes to 4.35%)

rate_differential_aud_usd.input: Widening vs USD (RBA 3.85% > Fed 3.50-3.75%; further hikes expected)

risk_sentiment.input: Risk-off (VIX 24.92, US-Iran war) - but AUD acting as safe haven per energy export status

china_growth.input: Stable/Mixed (Official NBS PMI 49.0 contraction vs private RatingDog PMI 52.1 expansion; China GDP target 4.5-5%)

6A.note: Total weighted 0 = NEUTRAL per table. Score set to +1 (SLIGHT_BULLISH PM code) given AUD/USD is at 0.7153 (near 52-week high) and AUD is outperforming as an energy exporter benefiting from elevated oil prices. BabyPips analysis notes AUD is acting as a 2026 safe haven. RBA hawkishness and widening rate differential provide structural support. Risk-off (weight 2, -2) is the key offset. Confidence 5/10 due to conflicting signals.

boj_stance.input: Hawkish/Tightening (BoJ at 0.75%, 30-year high; April hike to 1.00% expected per Bloomberg/Reuters polls)

rate_differential_jpy_usd.input: Narrowing vs USD (BoJ hiking while Fed holds; US-Japan spread compressing toward 2.75%)

6J.note: Total weighted +5 = STRONG_BULLISH per table (>=+5). Score set to 3 (PM code for STRONG_BULLISH). HIGHEST CONVICTION TRADE. BoJ hawkish pivot (weight 2, +2) + narrowing JPY-USD rate differential (weight 2, +2) + risk-off safe-haven bid (+1) = strong JPY bull case. USD/JPY at 158.30 - bullish 6J = bearish USD/JPY. All 51 economists expect BoJ hold next week but April hike widely expected. Signal decay: gradual (BoJ hiking cycle unfolds over Q2 2026).

key_drivers: DFII10 real yields confirmed FALLING (1.82%, down from ~2.0% in Jan) - key bullish shift for GC, SI, NQ vs 0230 session
US-Iran war driving geopolitical risk premium in crude oil (WTI $90.77, +17% from pre-war) and safe-haven demand for gold and JPY
BoJ hawkish pivot (0.75%, April hike expected) + narrowing JPY-USD differential = STRONG_BULLISH 6J (highest conviction trade)
VIX rising to 24.92 (+2.85%) and MOVE index at 78.59 (+23.54% 1mo) = elevated rate and equity volatility headwind for indices
RBA hawkish (3.85%, more hikes expected) + AUD as energy exporter safe haven = SLIGHT_BULLISH 6A despite risk-off

stale_sources: FRED DFII10 (latest observation Mar 10, 2026 - 2 business days old; within acceptable range per methodology)

TradingView NASDAQ:SOX for semiconductor index (7,914.56, +0.63% day)

World Gold Council / SSGA for gold ETF flows (Feb 2026 data)
data_quality.confidence_notes: DFII10 is 2 business days old but within the 1-2 day lag expected for FRED data. Real yield direction (FALLING) is confirmed by the trend from ~2.0% in Jan to 1.82% now. Gold ETF flows: early March saw some outflows (GLD -$4.58B per Seeking Alpha) but overall trend remains inflows (9 consecutive months, record AUM $701B). Scored as INFLOWS based on dominant multi-month trend.
